title: Fleet rename log v1
description: Canonical log of service-id and URL renames. Read by bin/generate.py (to emit `aliases`/`alias_urls` on the new entry), by fleet-runner nginx-render (to emit 301-redirect vhosts from the old hostname to the new one until retire_at), by fleet-runner audit-callers (to alert when a still-live caller hits an old URL), and by fleet-runner audit renames-active (to flag entries whose retire_at has passed so the redirect vhost can be torn down). One entry per discrete rename event; chains (A -> B -> C) are written as two entries (A->B and B->C), not flattened.

from_id.description: Pre-rename registry id (slug). The new entry's `aliases` field will include this value so by-id lookups don't 404.

to_id.description: Post-rename registry id. Must currently exist in services.json.

from_url.description: Pre-rename public hostname. Used by nginx-render to emit the 301-redirect vhost.

retire_at.description: When the 301-redirect vhost should be torn down. Optional — when absent the redirect lives indefinitely. Convention: 30 days from renamed_at unless a stakeholder request bumps it.

reason.description: One-line why. Future maintainers should read this and understand whether the rename can be reversed.

status.description: redirect=301 vhost active; retired=old vhost removed (returns 404); blocked=old hostname intentionally returns 410 Gone (e.g. compromised key, abandon URL).

broke_callers.description: Slug list of services that hard-coded the old URL and needed source patches when the rename happened. Empty when go-fleet-graph + grep confirmed no callers.

renamed_by.description: Free-form attribution (operator name, AI session, automation tag).
